cot_prefix: Okay, I need to figure out the main task of the IARC based on the provided text. Let me read through the passage again to make sure I understand the context. The article starts by stating that the International Agency for Research on Cancer (IARC) classifies air pollution as a Group 1 carcinogen. Then it mentions that the IARC's most widely used system for classifying carcinogens has five groups. The passage quotes Dana Loomis from IARC saying, "Our task was to evaluate the air everyone breathes rather than focus on specific air pollutants." So, their task is about evaluating the air in general, not specific pollutants. Later, it talks about how the IARC's conclusions apply globally. The director, Christopher Wild, emphasizes the need for international action based on their findings. So, the main task of IARC seems to be assessing whether air pollution causes cancer. Looking at the answer choices: A) To focus on cancer-causing substances. Well, they do classify carcinogens, but the quote from Loomis says they evaluated the air overall, not just specific substances. So maybe this isn't the main task. B) To evaluate air quality. The quote says they evaluated the air everyone breathes. But does "air quality" mean the same as the cancer risk from air? The IARC's role here is specifically about carcinogens, so evaluating the air in terms of cancer risk. But the answer choices might not
